title: Soothing Remedies for a Queasy Stomach with 7 Top Herbal Essential Oils

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for a queasy stomach because it helps to calm the stomach and reduce nausea by slowing down digestion and increasing blood flow to the stomach area.
Shogaol is good for a queasy stomach because it helps to reduce nausea and vomiting by relaxing the stomach muscles and improving digestion.
Zingiberene is good for queasy stomach because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm and soothe the stomach, reducing nausea and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oil, also known as ginger oil, is a natural remedy for a queasy stomach. It contains compounds that help to calm nausea and vomiting. Ginger oil has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the digestive tract, which can cause stomach pain and discomfort. It also helps to regulate digestion and relieve cramps. When inhaled, the oil's vapors stimulate the body's natural digestive processes, making it easier to absorb food. Additionally, ginger oil has a soothing effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that may be contributing to stomach queasiness. When applied topically, the oil's warming properties can also help to relieve stomach spasms and ease discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils that help with a queasy stomach, you will need some fresh lemongrass leaves and a few basic tools. Start by washing the lemongrass leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, chop the leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife, making sure to only use the leaves and not the woody stems. Then, fill a clean glass container with the chopped lemongrass leaves, leaving about an inch of space at the top. Pour a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil over the lemongrass leaves, making sure they are completely covered. The general ratio is one part lemongrass to two parts carrier oil. Now, place the container in a cool, dark place and let it infuse for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container every day. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. Your Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oil is now ready to use, but make sure to store it in a cool, dark place and use it in moderation, as it can be quite strong.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for queasy stomach

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anethole is good for a queasy stomach because it has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing nausea and discomfort caused by indigestion and other gastrointestinal issues.
Foeniculin is good for a queasy stomach because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the stomach, reducing nausea and discomfort.

Polyphenols are good for queasy stomach because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to relax the stomach muscles and reduce nausea, making them a natural remedy for soothing an upset stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel essential oil, is a natural remedy that can help with a queasy stomach. This is because it contains compounds that can ease digestion and reduce nausea. The oil has been traditionally used to calm stomach upset and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). When inhaled, the active compounds in fennel essential oil can help relax the stomach muscles and reduce spasms, which can help alleviate feelings of queasiness and discomfort. Additionally, fennel oil has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and promote healthy digestion. This natural remedy is often used to calm stomach upset during pregnancy, motion sickness, and other conditions that can cause a queasy stomach.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils that help with a queasy stomach, you'll need fresh lemongrass leaves, a clean glass jar, a cheesecloth, and a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. First, harvest fresh and healthy Cymbopogon citratus leaves from your garden or purchase them from a local herb store. Then, chop the leaves into small pieces and place them in the clean glass jar. Next, pour a carrier oil over the chopped leaves, making sure they are completely covered. Now, seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the oil infuse with the lemongrass essence. After the infusion period, strain the oil mixture through the cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids. Finally, transfer the Cymbopogon citratus essential oil to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool place, ready to use as a natural remedy for a queasy stomach.

Rosmarinic acid is good for queasy stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and relax the stomach muscles, making it easier to digest food and alleviate stomach discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oil, also known as peppermint oil, is great for a queasy stomach. The oil's main ingredient, menthol, helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and calm the digestive system. When you smell peppermint oil, it sends a signal to your brain that says "everything is okay" and helps to reduce nausea and vomiting. The oil also has a cooling effect, which can help to ease stomach cramps and make you feel better. Additionally, peppermint oil can help to stimulate digestion, which can help to move food through your system and reduce queasiness. Many people find that using peppermint oil in aromatherapy can help to relieve stomach upset and make them feel more comfortable.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils that help with a queasy stomach, start by gathering fresh lemongrass leaves from your garden or a local market. Choose healthy, green leaves with no signs of wilting or damage. Next, wash the leaves thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or bacteria. Then, dry the leaves using a clean towel or by placing them in a single layer on a paper towel, allowing them to air dry for several hours or overnight. Once the leaves are completely dry, place them in a clean glass jar with a lid. Fill a small saucepan with water, and add the dried lemongrass leaves to the water. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer for 20-30 minutes to extract the essential oils from the lemongrass. After the water has cooled slightly, strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container, discarding the solids. Let the liquid sit for a few hours or overnight to allow the essential oils to separate from the water. Finally, use a pipette or a dropper to carefully extract the essential oils from the top layer of the liquid and transfer them to a clean glass bottle. This homemade lemongrass essential oil can be stored in a cool, dark place and used as a natural remedy to soothe a queasy stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for queasy stomach

Flavonoids, also known as lemon grass, are good for a queasy stomach because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the digestive system and reduce nausea, making them a natural remedy for people with motion sickness or upset tummies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil, also known as licorice root oil, is a natural remedy for a queasy stomach. This oil is extracted from the roots of the licorice plant, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The active compounds in licorice root oil, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation. When ingested, these compounds can calm the digestive system and reduce nausea and vomiting. Additionally, licorice root oil has been shown to have a protective effect on the stomach lining, preventing damage from acid reflux and other stomach problems. By using licorice root oil, individuals can help alleviate symptoms of a queasy stomach, promoting a sense of calm and relief. The oil can be inhaled, taken orally, or added to a warm bath for relaxation and relief from stomach discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils that can help with queasy stomach, you will need fresh lemongrass leaves or Cymbopogon citratus stalks. First, harvest the lemongrass leaves from a healthy plant, making sure they are free from any signs of disease or pests. Next, gently wash the lemongrass leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris. Then, dry the lemongrass leaves completely using a clean towel or by air-drying them. Once the lemongrass leaves are dry, you can steam distill them to extract the essential oil. To do this, place the dried lemongrass leaves in a distillation apparatus and steam them with water. The steam will help to release the essential oil from the lemongrass leaves, which will then be collected in a separate container. Finally, filter the essential oil to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle. Cymbopogon citratus essential oil is known for its calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe a queasy stomach and reduce nausea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Anethum graveolens, also known as dill essential oil, has natural properties that can help soothe a queasy stomach. It contains compounds that work to calm digestive issues and reduce nausea. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help ease stomach cramps and discomfort. Additionally, dill essential oil has been known to aid in digestion by stimulating the production of digestive enzymes, which can help break down food more efficiently. This can be especially helpful when dealing with bloating or discomfort after eating. When inhaled, the oil's aroma can also help to calm the mind and body, which can further alleviate queasiness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils that help with a queasy stomach, you will need a few ingredients and some basic equipment. Start by gathering fresh lemongrass leaves, which are the leaves of the Cymbopogon citratus plant. You will need about 1 kilogram of fresh leaves, depending on the strength of oil you want to make. Next, clean and dry the leaves thoroughly to remove any dirt or moisture. Then, place the leaves in a large glass jar or container and cover them with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil. The ratio of leaves to oil is usually around 1:4, so for 1 kilogram of leaves, you will need 4 liters of oil. Seal the jar tightly and leave it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the oil extract from the leaves. After the extraction period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. Finally, you can use the lemongrass essential oil as needed to help alleviate a queasy stomach, by applying it to the skin, inhaling it, or adding it to a warm bath.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oil that helps with a queasy stomach, you will need some fresh lemongrass leaves, a carrier oil, and a clean glass jar. First, choose fresh and healthy lemongrass leaves, and wash them thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, dry the lemongrass leaves in the shade or by air-drying them for a few hours to remove excess moisture. Then, chop the dried lemongrass leaves into small pieces and fill a clean glass jar about a quarter full. Pour a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, over the lemongrass leaves, making sure they are completely covered. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar gently every day to help release the oil from the lemongrass leaves. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oil is now ready to use, but make sure to dilute it with a carrier oil before applying it to the skin, as it can be quite strong.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oil, also known as lemongrass oil, is a natural remedy for queasy stomach. The oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the digestive system. When inhaled, it can ease nausea and reduce vomiting. The oil also has a calming effect on the mind, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that often cause stomach upset. Additionally, lemongrass oil can help stimulate digestion and relieve cramps, making it a great natural remedy for people who experience stomach discomfort after eating. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oil that helps with a queasy stomach, you will need some fresh lemongrass leaves, a steam distillation apparatus, and a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. First, harvest the fresh lemongrass leaves in the early morning, when the oil content is highest. Then, wash the leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, use a steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil from the lemongrass leaves. This process involves heating the water in the distillation apparatus, which produces steam that passes through the lemongrass leaves, releasing the essential oil into the water. The steam and essential oil mixture is then cooled, and the essential oil is collected and separated from the water. The collected essential oil can be mixed with a carrier oil to create a herbal essential oil blend that can be used to help alleviate nausea and queasy stomachs. The recommended usage is to apply a few drops of the blend to the wrists or temples, inhale the aroma, or mix with water for a foot bath.

intro_desc: If you often feel queasy or nauseous, using herbal essential oils can be a great help. This is because certain herbs have natural properties that can calm your stomach and make you feel better. Two such herbs are Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, and Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel. Ginger oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe an upset stomach, while fennel oil has a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting. Fixing this issue is important because a queasy stomach can disrupt your daily life, making it hard to eat, work, or even sleep. It's not just about feeling uncomfortable; a persistent queasy stomach can also lead to dehydration, weight loss, and fatigue. By using herbal essential oils like ginger and fennel, you can alleviate your symptoms and improve your overall well-being. You can do this by inhaling the oils, applying them to your skin, or adding them to your bath water. Using these oils can also help you to feel more confident and in control, which can have a positive impact on your mental health and quality of life.
supplementary_best: A combination of three herbal essential oils can help remedy a queasy stomach. The best combination is Foeniculum vulgare (fennel), Mentha x piperita (peppermint), and Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice root). Fennel oil helps to soothe the digestive system and ease cramps in the stomach. Peppermint oil has a calming effect on the stomach muscles, reducing nausea and discomfort. Licorice root oil, with its anti-inflammatory properties, helps to protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to queasiness. This combination works well together because it addresses the physical discomfort and inflammation associated with a queasy stomach, while also providing a calming effect to help reduce nausea and cramps.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a queasy stomach, it's best to avoid certain herbal essential oils that can make symptoms worse. Peppermint oil (Mentha piperita isn't mentioned, but Peppermint's relative, 'Mentha x piperita' isn't mentioned, so 'Mentha arvensis' is the alternative), which can be too stimulating, should be avoided. Another oil to watch out for is 'Cinchona officinalis', also known as Quinine oil, as it can irritate the stomach. 'Cedrus deodora' or 'Cedarwood oil' may not be suitable either, as it can cause digestive issues in some people. 'Juniperus communis' or 'Juniper oil' can be too intense for a queasy stomach. 'Melissa officinalis' or 'Lemon Balm oil' can be irritating if not used carefully, so it's best to approach with caution. Additionally, 'Citrus aurantium' or 'Bitter Orange oil' can also be too potent for someone with a sensitive stomach. These oils can be too intense or irritating, and may make queasiness worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: Ginger has long been used as a natural remedy to help with queasy stomach. Its active compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea. When consumed as a tea, ginger can be especially effective in soothing an upset stomach. This is because the steam from the hot water releases the ginger's active compounds into the air, allowing them to be easily absorbed by the body. Additionally, ginger has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.  Another natural remedy that can help with queasy stomach is aloe vera juice. Aloe vera contains compounds that can help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. Drinking aloe vera juice can help to calm an upset stomach and reduce nausea. This is because the juice contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach. Aloe vera juice can also help to restore the balance of good bacteria in the gut, which can become imbalanced when the digestive system is upset.  Fennel seeds have also been used for centuries to help with queasy stomach. Fennel seeds contain compounds that can help to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea. When consumed as a tea, fennel seeds can be especially effective in soothing an upset stomach. This is because the steam from the hot water releases the fennel's active compounds into the air, allowing them to be easily absorbed by the body. Fennel seeds also contain anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.  Probiotics, found in foods such as yogurt and kefir, can also help to alleviate symptoms of a queasy stomach. Probiotics are live bacteria and yeasts that are beneficial for the digestive system. When taken as a supplement or consumed through food, probiotics can help to restore the balance of good bacteria in the gut, which can become imbalanced when the digestive system is upset. This can help to alleviate symptoms of nausea and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have queasy stomach often experience other ailments, including digestive issues like bloating, gas, and diarrhea. This is because their stomach is sensitive and easily irritated, making it harder for food to digest properly. Medicinal herbs like ginger, peppermint, and fennel can help alleviate these symptoms. Ginger, for example, has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the stomach and reduce nausea. Peppermint oil can help relax the muscles in the stomach and improve digestion, while fennel seeds can ease bloating and cramps. These herbs work by reducing inflammation, calming the stomach muscles, and improving digestion, making it easier for the body to absorb nutrients.  Some people who have queasy stomach may also experience stress and anxiety, as the physical discomfort can be mentally distressing. Herbs like chamomile, lavender, and passionflower can help calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. Chamomile, for instance, has a soothing effect on the nervous system, while lavender can help regulate sleep patterns. Passionflower can reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calm. These herbs work by interacting with the brain's neurotransmitters, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.  Additionally, people with queasy stomach may experience fatigue, as their body is expending energy to deal with the digestive issues. Herbs like ashwagandha and ginseng can help boost energy levels and reduce fatigue. Ashwagandha, for example, is an adaptogen that can help the body adapt to stress, while ginseng can improve circulation and increase energy. These herbs work by improving circulation, regulating blood sugar levels, and reducing inflammation, making it easier for the body to function properly.  Lastly, people who have queasy stomach may also experience skin issues like acne and rashes, as their body is producing more inflammation and toxins. Herbs like turmeric and dandelion can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. Turmeric, for example, contains curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound, while dandelion can help cleanse the blood and reduce toxins. These herbs work by reducing inflammation, promoting healing, and cleansing the body of toxins.
